Source focus: Lemma lem.det.minors-diag: For a diagonal n×n matrix D with entries d_i, (a) det(sub_P^P D) = ∏_{i∈P} d_i for any subset P of [n]; (b) if P and Q are distinct subsets of [n] with |P|=|Q|, then det(sub_P^Q D)=0.
Formalization sketch:

- Statement: For n:ℕ, d: Fin n → R, let D := Matrix.diagonal d : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R. Then (a) for any P : Finset (Fin n), det (sub[P,P] D) = ∏ i in P, d i; (b) for any P Q : Finset (Fin n), P ≠ Q → P.card = Q.card → det (sub[P,Q] D) = 0.

- Proof plan: (a) sub[P,P] D is itself diagonal with entries d_i for i∈P, so its determinant is the product of those entries. (b) Since |P|=|Q| and P≠Q, there exists i∈P\Q; the row of sub[P,Q] D corresponding to that i is zero, hence determinant zero.
Selected candidate Mathlib/API context:
- `Matrix.det_diagonal`; shape: det (diagonal v) = ∏ i, v i; why: to compute det of the diagonal submatrix in part (a); confidence: 0.9
- `Matrix.diagonal_apply`; shape: diagonal v i j = if i=j then v i else 0; why: to reason about entries of D; confidence: 0.9
- `Finset.card_eq_iff_eq_empty_of_subset`; shape: s ⊆ t → (s.card = t.card ↔ s = t); why: to deduce existence of i∈P\Q from P≠Q and equal cardinalities; confidence: 0.5
- `Finset.exists_mem_not_mem_of_ne`; shape: s ≠ t → ∃ a, a ∈ s ∧ a ∉ t; why: to get i∈P\Q; confidence: 0.7
Proof notes:
- Use submatrixOfFinset_apply to relate entries of sub[P,Q] D to D entries.
- For part (b), after finding i∈P\Q, show that row i of sub[P,Q] D is zero because D i j = 0 for all j (since D is diagonal and i≠j for j∈Q).
- The determinant of a matrix with a zero row is zero (Matrix.det_eq_zero_of_row_eq_zero).
Uncertainties to resolve during repair/review:
- Exact name of lemma that a matrix with a zero row has zero determinant: likely Matrix.det_eq_zero_of_row_eq_zero or similar.
- Whether Finset.exists_mem_not_mem_of_ne is available in Mathlib; may need to use Finset.ssubset_iff_subset_ne and Finset.exists_mem_not_mem.
Hydrated Mathlib source snippets:

Mathlib source match for `Matrix.det_diagonal` at Mathlib/LinearAlgebra/Matrix/Determinant/Basic.lean:76:
```lean
theorem det_diagonal {d : n → R} : det (diagonal d) = ∏ i, d i := by
  rw [det_apply']
  refine (Finset.sum_eq_single 1 ?_ ?_).trans ?_
  · rintro σ - h2
    obtain ⟨x, h3⟩ := not_forall.1 (mt Equiv.ext h2)
    convert mul_zero (ε σ)
    apply Finset.prod_eq_zero (mem_univ x)
    exact if_neg h3
  · simp
  · simp
```

Mathlib source match for `Matrix.diagonal_apply` at Mathlib/Combinatorics/HalesJewett.lean:332:
```lean
theorem diagonal_apply {α ι} [Nonempty ι] (x : α) : diagonal α ι x = fun _ => x := by
  ext; simp [diagonal]

/-- The **Hales-Jewett theorem**. This version has a restriction on universe levels which is
necessary for the proof. See `exists_mono_in_high_dimension` for a fully universe-polymorphic
version. -/
```

```lean
  rfl

@[simp]
```

Source focus: Lemma lem.sf.alternant-0: For α: Fin N → ℕ, (a) if α has two equal entries then a_α = 0; (b) if β is obtained from α by swapping two entries, then a_β = -a_α.
Formalization sketch:
- Statement: (a) If α i = α j for some i≠j, then alternant α = 0. (b) For any transposition τ = (i j), alternant (α ∘ τ) = - alternant α.

- Proof plan: (a) The sum over permutations defining alternant pairs each σ with σ∘τ, where τ swaps the two equal indices; the two terms cancel because sign(σ∘τ) = -sign(σ) and xPow(α∘(σ∘τ)) = xPow(α∘σ) since α has equal entries at i,j. (b) Directly from the definition: alternant (α∘τ) = ∑_σ sign(σ) xPow(α∘τ∘σ) = ∑_σ sign(σ∘τ) xPow(α∘σ) = -∑_σ sign(σ) xPow(α∘σ) = -alternant α.
Selected candidate Mathlib/API context:
- `Equiv.Perm.sign_swap`; shape: sign (swap i j) = -1 if i≠j; why: to get sign(σ∘τ) = -sign(σ); confidence: 0.9
- `Equiv.Perm.sign_mul`; shape: sign (σ * τ) = sign σ * sign τ; why: to compute sign of composition; confidence: 0.9
- `Finset.sum_involution`; shape: sum_involution f h ...; why: to pair terms in the sum for part (a); confidence: 0.8
- `Equiv.Perm.mul_swap_involutive`; shape: mul_swap_involutive i j σ; why: to show the involution property; confidence: 0.7
Proof notes:
- For part (a), use Finset.sum_involution with σ ↦ σ * Equiv.swap i j.
- Need to show xPow (α ∘ (σ * swap i j)) = xPow (α ∘ σ) when α i = α j. This follows from xPow being invariant under permuting equal entries.
- For part (b), reindex the sum by σ' = σ∘τ⁻¹.
- The local definition of alternant uses Equiv.Perm.sign σ • xPow (α ∘ σ); note the scalar multiplication •.
Uncertainties to resolve during repair/review:
- Whether there is a lemma xPow_comp_perm that relates xPow (α ∘ σ) to xPow α under permutation; may need to unfold xPow definition.
- Exact name of the lemma that sign(swap i j) = -1 when i≠j: likely Equiv.Perm.sign_swap.

Mathlib source match for `Equiv.Perm.sign_mul` at Mathlib/Data/EReal/Inv.lean:103:
```lean
theorem sign_mul (x y : EReal) : sign (x * y) = sign x * sign y := by
  induction x, y using induction₂_symm_neg with
  | top_zero => simp only [mul_zero, sign_zero]
  | top_top => rfl
  | symm h => rwa [mul_comm, EReal.mul_comm]
  | coe_coe => simp only [← coe_mul, sign_coe, _root_.sign_mul]
  | top_pos _ h =>
    rw [top_mul_coe_of_pos h, sign_top, one_mul, sign_pos (EReal.coe_pos.2 h)]
  | neg_left h => rw [neg_mul, sign_neg, sign_neg, h, neg_mul]

@[simp] protected theorem sign_mul_abs : ∀ x : EReal, (sign x * x.abs : EReal) = x
  | ⊥ => by simp
  | ⊤ => by simp
  | (x : ℝ) => by rw [sign_coe, coe_abs, ← coe_coe_sign, ← coe_mul, sign_mul_abs]

@[simp] protected theorem abs_mul_sign (x : EReal) : (x.abs * sign x : EReal) = x := by
  rw [EReal.mul_comm, EReal.sign_mul_abs]
```

```lean
theorem sign_mul (x y : α) : sign (x * y) = sign x * sign y := by
  rcases lt_trichotomy x 0 with (hx | hx | hx) <;> rcases lt_trichotomy y 0 with (hy | hy | hy) <;>
    simp [hx, hy, mul_pos_of_neg_of_neg, mul_neg_of_neg_of_pos, mul_neg_of_pos_of_neg]

@[simp] theorem sign_mul_abs (x : α) : (sign x * |x| : α) = x := by
  rcases lt_trichotomy x 0 with hx | rfl | hx <;> simp [*, abs_of_pos, abs_of_neg]

@[simp] theorem abs_mul_sign (x : α) : (|x| * sign x : α) = x := by
  rcases lt_trichotomy x 0 with hx | rfl | hx <;> simp [*, abs_of_pos, abs_of_neg]

@[simp]
```
